title:
Clinical Scanning
Workflow
subtitle: Redesigning the clinical scanning workflow to improve speed, clarity, and decision-making in real treatment conditions.

slides.title:
Clinical Scanning
Workflow
slides.description: Align Technology powers digital dentistry chair-side. Their scanning workflow — RX, Scan, and View — had friction at every phase. We redesigned all three into one continuous system.

slides.label: The System
slides.title: A system used during live patient treatment
slides.content: iTero is used chair-side while the patient is in the room. Three phases — RX, Scan, and View — feed directly into each other. A breakdown in any one affects the entire appointment.
issues: RX → define patient, procedure, and case details
View → validate results before lab submission
slides.highlight: Every decision happens under time pressure — with the patient in the chair.

slides.label: The Problem
slides.title: The system grew. The experience didn't.
slides.content: RX, Scan, and View were built up over time with no shared logic. What should feel like one workflow became three disconnected systems.
issues: Setup blocked users before they could start scanning
Procedure and tooth selection required too much mental effort
Scanning stopped every time a tool needed adjusting
Nothing signalled when a scan was actually complete

slides.label: User Research
slides.title: What we heard in the room
slides.content: We observed clinicians during live sessions and followed up with interviews. The same frustrations surfaced everywhere — accepted as part of the job, not reported as problems.

quotes.text: The patient popup interrupts me before I even start.
quotes.author: Dr. Yael Levi

quotes.text: I change the procedure multiple times before I even begin scanning.
quotes.author: Dr. Amir Cohen

quotes.text: The moment I go looking for a tool, I lose my rhythm completely.
quotes.author: Dr. Noa Ben-David

quotes.text: I never fully trust the result the first time I see it.
quotes.author: Dr. Efrat Mizrahi

quotes.text: Even when I'm done, I keep going back to check.
quotes.author: Dr. Daniel Katz

quotes.text: I never know if I'm selecting the right tooth.
quotes.author: Dr. Lior Haddad

slides.label: Design Goals
slides.title: Four problems. Four things to get right.

goals.title: Let clinicians start without friction
goals.description: Remove the setup gates that block users before a scan can even begin.

goals.title: Keep the scanning motion unbroken
goals.description: Stop the interface from interrupting the physical act of scanning.

goals.title: Make every tool self-explanatory
goals.description: Clarity built in — not something users figure out over time.

goals.title: Make results trustworthy on first view
goals.description: A clear signal that the scan is done — no need to keep checking.

kpis: Time from RX entry to first scan action

slides.label: Interactive Prototype
slides.title: Testing decisions before they became real
slides.content: We built an interactive prototype using Claude Code to simulate the real scanning workflow — testing decisions under clinical conditions before anything reached development.
bullets2: Real workflow simulation — not static screens
Changes tested the same day they were made
slides.highlight: Prototyping in code let us test things a Figma prototype simply can't — continuous motion, real-time feedback, and tool sequencing.

image.caption: Interactive prototype built with Claude Code

slides.title: RX
slides.subtitle: The prescription step where clinicians define the patient, procedure, and case details before scanning begins.

slides.label: RX Setup
slides.title: Getting started shouldn't feel like a form

afterImage.caption: New — inline setup, immediate scan access

slides.beforeDescription: A rigid setup sequence blocked users before scanning could begin — no shortcuts, no flexibility.
slides.afterDescription: Users start immediately and fill in context as they go.
beforeBullets: Patient selection required a multi-step modal
Procedure had to be defined upfront — no flexibility
Dropdown disconnected from the dental chart
afterBullets: Inline patient selection — no modal, no interruption
Procedure selection visual and tied to teeth structure

slides.label: RX Tooth Selection
slides.title: Selecting teeth should be as fast as pointing at them

beforeImage.caption: Old — one modal per tooth, no overview

afterImage.caption: New — visual chart, multi-select, color-coded procedures

slides.beforeDescription: Every tooth required its own modal — click, confirm, close, repeat. No overview, no way to see the full treatment plan.
slides.afterDescription: Teeth selected directly on the chart — individually or in groups — with procedures assigned inline.
beforeBullets: One blocking modal per tooth — no multi-select
No color coding — no clinical overview
Complex cases required many repetitive steps
afterBullets: Select one or many teeth in a single interaction
Color-coded procedures visible on the chart
Full treatment plan visible without opening anything

slides.title: Scan & View
slides.subtitle: The active phase where clinicians capture a 3D model in real time — then analyse, validate, and decide before anything reaches the lab.

slides.label: Scan Toolbar
slides.title: From scattered icons to a system users can learn once

beforeImage.caption: Old — mixed styles, no consistent location

afterImage.caption: New — unified icons in a single collapsible toolbar

slides.beforeDescription: Icons accumulated from different sources with no shared style — and tools had no permanent home. Users had no pattern to rely on mid-scan.
slides.afterDescription: A unified icon set and a collapsible toolbar — consistent language, fixed location, labelled on demand.
beforeBullets: No shared visual language across icons
No dedicated place for tools — each lived somewhere different
Every new feature added more scatter
afterBullets: Unified icon set — each maps clearly to its action
One collapsible toolbar, always in the same place
Scalable — new tools slot in without disruption

slides.label: Multi-scan & Compare
slides.title: Managing multiple scans — and comparing them
slides.content: Clinicians capture pre-treatment, post-treatment, and additional references — then need to compare them. Tabs handle the organisation; the multi-layer panel handles the comparison.
bullets2: Add and switch between scans within one session
Overlay scans and adjust opacity in real time
slides.highlight: What used to be a mental task became a visual action you can do in seconds.

slides.label: View Tools
slides.title: From icon bars to clinical decision panels

beforeImage.caption: Old — unlabelled icon clusters, no hierarchy

afterImage.caption: New — named panels with one primary CTA per tool

slides.beforeDescription: Unlabelled icon clusters with no hierarchy — doctors memorised what every icon did and figured out the sequence themselves.
slides.afterDescription: Named panels with one clear primary action. Same structure across all tools — doctors build muscle memory fast.
beforeBullets: Unlabelled icons — meaning had to be memorised
No hierarchy — all actions carried equal weight
Destructive actions mixed with primary ones
afterBullets: Named panels with a single primary CTA per tool
AI detect is the default — doctors review, not draw

slides.label: Design System
slides.title: The work became the system
slides.content: The patterns created throughout the redesign were formalised into a design system — published in Figma and built in Claude Code as a living developer reference.
slides.highlight: The design influenced the system built after it — and became a permanent part of it.

image.caption: Design system components formalised from the redesign

slides.label: Outcomes
slides.title: Four friction points. Four things that got better.

outcomes.title: Time to start scanning
outcomes.description: No setup gates — clinicians start the moment they're ready.

outcomes.title: Interruptions mid-scan
outcomes.description: A consistent toolbar meant doctors stopped searching and stayed in flow.

outcomes.title: Tools and states
outcomes.description: Named panels replaced icon clusters — no memorisation required.

outcomes.title: Confidence in scan results
outcomes.description: Clear completion signals meant doctors trusted results on first view.
